title: descurainia pinnata var. brachycarpon
intro: Descurainia pinnata var. brachycarpon, also known as western wallflower or American wallflower, is a biennial plant native to western North America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its high content of isothiocyanates, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is also believed to have health benefits, including antioxidant and immunomodulatory properties, which may help protect against oxidative stress and boost the immune system. Traditionally, western wallflower is prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
